sections.heading: These Rules may be cited as the Subordinate Courts (Civil Short title
The jurisdiction of a Subordinate Court in all personal suits Jurisdiction
sections.text:
whether arising from contract, or from tort or from both, where of
Subordinate
the value of the property, debt or damage claimed, whether as Courts in
balance of accounts or otherwise is, where the court is presided personal
suits
over by a —
(a) chief resident magistrate, not more than one million
kwacha;
(b) principal resident magistrate, not more than nine hundred
thousand kwacha;
(c) senior resident magistrate, not more that five hundred
thousand kwacha;
(d) resident magistrate, not more than three hundred thousand
kwacha;
(e) magistrate of the first class, not more than one hundred
and fifty thousand kwacha;
(f) magistrate of the second class, not more than one hundred
thousand kwacha; and
(g) magistrate of the third class, not more than seventy-five
thousand kwacha.

sections.heading: The jurisdiction of a Subordinate Court of the first class in Civil
sections.text:
an action for the recovery for land is only to be exercised where— jurisdiction
of

(c) in case of a Subordinate Court presided over by a chief
resident magistrate, principal resident magistrate or a
senior resident magistrate, the rent payable in respect
thereof is not more than five hundred thousand kwacha
in a year.
M. MALILA,
LUSAKA Chief Justice
